# Datasheet URL Repair Campaign Report

**Date:** 2026-06-13  
**Generated by:** run_datasheet_url_repair.py + targeted enhancement passes

---

## CRITICAL: Patches Require Overwrite-Mode Application

The standard `apply_patches.py` is **fill-only** — it skips fields that already have a value.
The `datasheetUrl` field exists in every row targeted by this campaign (filled with a bad value).
**The orchestrator must run these patches in overwrite mode**, not fill mode.

Recommended approach: modify `apply_patches.py` to accept a `--overwrite` flag for these
URL-repair patches, or write a dedicated `apply_datasheet_url_patches.py` that unconditionally
replaces `datasheetUrl` when the patch MPN matches and the current value matches a bad pattern.

---

## Census: Bad URLs in TAS (before patching)

| Category | Total rows | Bad-URL rows | vishay-search | datasheetpdf | example.com |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| capacitors | 112,231 | 37,616 (33.5%) | 37,616 | 0 | 0 |
| resistors | 117,472 | 86,751 (73.8%) | 86,751 | 0 | 0 |
| mosfets | 6,678 | 2,526 (37.8%) | 1,967 | 559 | 0 |
| diodes | 3,189 | 1,481 (46.4%) | 321 | 502 | 658 |
| igbts | 2,075 | 288 (13.9%) | 33 | 223 | 32 |
| magnetics | 50,556 | 5,209 (10.3%) | 5,191 | 0 | 18 |
| **TOTAL** | **292,201** | **133,871 (45.8%)** | **131,879** | **1,284** | **708** |

Bad-URL patterns per `heaviside/librarian/guards.py` `BAD_DATASHEET_URL_PATTERNS`:
- `vishay.com/en/search` — Vishay product-search page (not a datasheet)
- `datasheetpdf.com` — third-party aggregator search page
- `example.com` — synthetic placeholder URL (bulk-generated rows)

---

## Resolution Methods and Verification

### Method 1: Vishay Predictive API

**Endpoint:** `https://www.vishay.com/api/search-predictive/?searchChoice=part&query=<mpn>`

**Algorithm:**
1. Query MPN (with fallback queries: strip `D/` prefix, strip ` e3` suffix, try series prefix, try comma-split tokens)
2. Filter `type=DATSHT` hits → extract `doc_no`, `p1001`
3. Construct PDF URL: `https://www.vishay.com/docs/<doc_no>/<strip_non_alnum(p1001)>.pdf`
4. If simple filename fails: scrape `https://www.vishay.com/en/product/<doc_no>/` and extract PDF link
5. HTTP HEAD verify: must return `Content-Type: application/pdf`

**Sample verification (20 MPNs per template before bulk run):**

| Category | Sample MPNs tested | Pass rate | Template |
|---|---|---|---|
| resistors (series names) | 20 | 80% → 93.2% with fallbacks | vishay.com/docs/`doc_no`/`p1001_fname`.pdf |
| capacitors (MAL* series) | 100 | 62% direct + 100% via 6-char series prefix | same |
| mosfets (Vishay Siliconix) | 20 | 98%+ | same |
| magnetics (Vishay Dale IH*) | 20 | 42 via API, 37 more via DigiKey | same + DigiKey |
| diodes (Vishay) | 20 | ~95% | same |
| igbts (Vishay) | 20 | 100% | same |

### Method 2: Digi-Key Product Information API v3

**Endpoint:** `GET /Search/v3/Products/Keywords` → `ExactManufacturerProducts[].PrimaryDatasheet`

Used for:
- `datasheetpdf.com` rows in mosfets, diodes, igbts (low hit rate ~1-2% from DigiKey searches)
- Vishay magnetics inductors (IHLP-* series, 50% hit rate)

**Note:** DigiKey hit rate for datasheetpdf.com semiconductor rows was very low (1-2%).
These MPNs (onsemi, ST, EPC, Infineon) are often EOL or DigiKey's exact-MPN search
doesn't match the TAS partNumber format. DigiKey rate limit was hit during the campaign
(429 with Retry-After: ~68min), limiting the magnetics DigiKey pass.

---

## Unresolved Remainder (19,088 rows) — Reasons

### Resistors: 25 unique MPNs, ~14,016 rows

Very old Vishay specialty/military series not in the Vishay predictive API. Examples:
`M25SI`, `G200`, `Z300`, `DCRF`, `PHT`, `PVHT`, `P-NS`, `L-NS`, `DLA Moisture Resistant Chip Resistor`.
These are obsolete military-spec and power wirewound resistors. No automated resolution path found.

### Mosfets: 558 unique MPNs, 558 rows

- **1 Vishay-search** (`SQM120P10-10m1L`): hyphenated MPN with lowercase variant not in API.
- **557 datasheetpdf.com**: Manufacturers: onsemi (129), STMicroelectronics (106), EPC (90),
  ROHM (55), Power Integrations (47), Transphorm (43), Infineon (38), Siliconix (25), Wolfspeed (14).
  These are real parts but not found in DigiKey exact search. Manufacturer websites (ST, Infineon)
  block server-side HTTP requests. **Recommended action**: download datasheets manually or
  use a browser-based fetcher for these 558 MPNs.

### Diodes: 1,155 unique MPNs, 1,155 rows

- **658 example.com** (Infineon 279, Wolfspeed 220, ROHM 146, STMicroelectronics 149, ON Semi 55):
  These are SiC Schottky Barrier Diodes from the legacy bulk-generation pass. The MPNs appear
  to be real but obsolete (not found in DigiKey, not on manufacturer product pages).
  **Cannot be resolved automatically.** These rows may need manual investigation or removal.
- **497 datasheetpdf.com** (Littelfuse 114, Infineon 105, Vishay 104, onsemi 63, Wolfspeed 63):
  DigiKey hit rate was 1% for this group. Same limitation as mosfets above.

### IGBTs: 255 unique MPNs, 255 rows

- **32 example.com** (Fuji Electric 15, Mitsubishi 14, Infineon 3):
  Synthetic placeholder URLs. Fuji Electric and Mitsubishi use non-standard URL schemes.
- **223 datasheetpdf.com** (onsemi 85, STMicroelectronics 50, Fuji Electric 33, ROHM 30):
  DigiKey returned 0 matches for all 223. These IGBTs may be EOL or use different DigiKey naming.

### Magnetics: 284 unique MPNs, 3,104 rows

- **266 Vishay-search** (Vishay inductors): The IHLP/IHCL/IHXL/IFSC/IHSR/IHLM series inductors
  from Vishay Dale are not indexed in the Vishay predictive API. DigiKey covered ~50% of tested
  MPNs but hit rate was 12% overall (37/303). The remaining ~226 Vishay inductor MPNs have no
  automated resolution path.
- **10 example.com Coilcraft**: `LPD6235C-*` series. Coilcraft website is behind Cloudflare
  protection; their datasheet URLs use random GUIDs. DigiKey returned no matches.
- **8 example.com TDK**: `SPM6530T-*`, `NLJ324ST-*` series. DigiKey returned no matches.

---

## Source Integrity

All emitted URLs were verified by either:
1. HTTP HEAD request returning `Content-Type: application/pdf`, OR
2. URL scraped directly from the manufacturer's product page HTML, OR  
3. DigiKey `PrimaryDatasheet` field (manufacturer-provided, not DigiKey-hosted)

**No URLs were guessed or constructed without verification.**  
Exception: DigiKey `PrimaryDatasheet` for Littelfuse `assetdocs` URLs return 403 from server-side
HEAD checks but are legitimate (64 such URLs already exist as accepted rows in the DB).
ST.com PDF URLs also timeout on server-side HEAD but are accepted from DigiKey as primary datasource.

---

## Patch File Locations

- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_capacitors.ndjson` — 9,389 patches
- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_resistors.ndjson` — 341 patches
- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_mosfets.ndjson` — 1,968 patches
- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_diodes.ndjson` — 154 patches
- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_igbts.ndjson` — 33 patches
- `scripts/enrichment/datasheet_url_patches_magnetics.ndjson` — 79 patches

**Total: 11,964 patch records**

Each patch record format:
```json
{"category":"<cat>","mpn":"<partNumber>","set":{"manufacturerInfo.datasheetUrl":"<new url>"},
 "source":"<new url>","evidence":"<verification method>"}
```

The `mpn` field matches `datasheetInfo.part.partNumber` in the TAS envelope body
(same key used by `apply_patches.py`).

---

## Application Instructions

> **These patches require overwrite-mode application.** The `datasheetUrl` field exists
> in every target row (with a bad value). Standard `apply_patches.py` will report these
> as `conflict` and leave them unchanged.

**Option A:** Modify `apply_patches.py` to add `--overwrite-url-repair` flag that, for
patches targeting `manufacturerInfo.datasheetUrl`, replaces the value unconditionally
when the current value matches a `BAD_DATASHEET_URL_PATTERNS` pattern.

**Option B:** Write `apply_datasheet_url_patches.py` that reads the `datasheet_url_patches_*.ndjson`
files and applies unconditional `datasheetUrl` overwrites, checking the bad-URL guard
before replacing.

Do NOT apply these patches with the current fill-only `apply_patches.py` — it will report
11,964 conflicts and make no changes.
